review_notes: The functions are mostly accurate and map well to the NIST control IA-5(7) regarding the management of authenticators. The focus on ensuring secrets and etcd are encrypted aligns with the control's intent to prevent unencrypted static authenticators. However, the function name 'k8s_secret_no_unencrypted_static_authenticators' was slightly ambiguous and was refined for clarity.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly specify the action being checked, such as 'embedded' in the context of static authenticators.
title: Authenticator Management - Authenticator management | no embedded unencrypted static authenticators ensure
step1_reasoning: The control IA-5(7) focuses on ensuring that authenticators are not embedded in an unencrypted static form. In Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that secrets are not stored in an unencrypted form and that etcd, which stores secrets, is encrypted. The functions focus on verifying that secrets are encrypted at rest and that no unencrypted static authenticators are embedded within Kubernetes secrets.
